name: Data Destruction (DRY-RUN only)
description: Impact / TA0040. The real technique is destructive deletion of files / volumes. This descriptor is DRY-RUN ONLY: it prints the path it WOULD target (`/tmp/sovereign-offense-harness-dryrun-target-DOES-NOT-EXIST`) and exits without invoking rm or any destructive syscall. Included so the coverage matrix has an honest Impact entry. The TTP runner's safety gate (refuse-by-default + --target whitelist) is structural here: even if the exec line were rewritten to invoke rm, the gate would still refuse without --unsafe-local.
exec: echo 'T1485 DRY-RUN: would target /tmp/sovereign-offense-harness-dryrun-target-DOES-NOT-EXIST'; echo 'no destructive syscall invoked'; echo 'no rm, no truncate, no >, no unlink'; ls /tmp/sovereign-offense-harness-dryrun-target-DOES-NOT-EXIST 2>&1 | head -1
